title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Repository Trace: Push Refspec Safety
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git push refspec safety increment in a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.
Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Push Refspec Safety
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local main is 3 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Push Refspec Safety contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Push Refspec Safety while producing a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local main is 3 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push. A defensible solution applies Push Refspec Safety, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-push-refspec-safety — Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.
git remote -v
git fetch --prune origin
git log --left-right --graph --oneline main...origin/main
git push --dry-run origin main:main
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git push always publishes the currently checked-out branch to a branch with the same name. This fails the Push Refspec Safety contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
